assertion_results.text: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, Step 2 (line 23) states: 'Search all returned comments for those whose body starts with the marker string `[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:`. This is the fixed marker defined in `shared/description-digest-protocol.md`.' Also in plan.md, Step 1.5 (line 42) states: 'Locate the digest comment by searching for the marker `[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:`.' Both files explicitly reference the marker string and the protocol document.

assertion_results.text: The response describes computing a digest of the current description (using scripts/sha256-digest.py or SHA-256) and comparing it with the stored digest
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, Step 5 (lines 48-58) describes computing the current digest: 'Extract the description field from the TC-9201 issue response. Write it to a temporary file and compute the digest: python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/desc-TC-9201.txt'. Step 7 (lines 71-76) performs the comparison: 'Stored hex: a1b2c3d4... Computed hex: a1b2c3d4... The digests match.' The plan.md Step 1.5 (lines 48-49) also confirms: 'Stored hex matches computed hex — digests match.'

assertion_results.text: The response confirms the digests match and states it will proceed silently without prompting the user — no additional latency for the happy path
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, line 80 states: 'Proceed silently. In plan.md, line 51 states: 'Proceed silently to Step 2 with no user prompt.'

assertion_results.text: The response does not alert the user or pause execution due to the digest check — it proceeds directly to subsequent steps

The skill moves directly to Step 2 (Verify Dependencies).'
